name: KAT7 inactivation alleviated senescence and extended aged mouse lifespan
summary: KAT7 is the strongest direct histone-acetyltransferase intervention signal in the baseline.
statement: KAT7 deficiency reduced histone H3 lysine 14 acetylation and senescence markers in progeroid human mesenchymal precursor cells, while intravenous Cas9/sg-Kat7 vectors alleviated liver aging and extended lifespan in physiologically aged and progeroid mice.
population_or_model: Progeroid human cells plus physiologically aged and Zmpste24-/- progeroid mice
time_horizon: Cellular senescence assays and mouse survival follow-up after systemic vector delivery
quantitative_note: The PubMed abstract reports lifespan extension but does not provide a percentage effect size.
caveats: This is gene-editing/vector evidence, not a validated drug or human therapy.
The human evidence is cell-based and progeroid, not an organismal aging trial.
Liver-aging and progeroid-model effects may not generalize across tissues or normal aging.
